name: empty_arch_satisfies_inv
code:
proof fn phi_1_empty_arch_satisfies_inv(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
    ensures
        arch.inv(),
{
}
reason: A zero-layer architecture cannot map any addresses and should not satisfy the invariant

name: aligned_trivial_size_one
reason: Alignment to size 1 is vacuously true for every address, providing no meaningful constraint

name: byte_granularity_arch_valid
code:
proof fn phi_3_byte_granularity_arch_valid(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.layers.len() == 1,
        arch.entry_size(0) == 1,
        arch.num_entries(0) == 1,
    ensures
        arch.inv(),
{
}
reason: An arch with 1-byte entry size and single entry covers only 1 byte, far below any valid x86 page size

name: zero_width_entry_region
code:
proof fn phi_4_zero_width_entry_region(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx) == arch.next_entry_base(layer, base, idx),
{
}
reason: Equal entry_base and next_entry_base means a zero-width address region, so distinct entries would alias

name: wellformed_implies_zero_entries
code:
proof fn phi_5_wellformed_implies_zero_entries(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.well_formed(),
    ensures
        d.num_entries() == 0,
{
}
reason: A well-formed directory with zero entries would make the page table structure degenerate and unmappable
